evals.prompt: Use CleanShot X to capture the active browser dashboard at desktop, wide desktop, and mobile sizes. Save each image under docs/screenshots and do the work unattended.
evals.expected_output: The agent distinguishes interactive from unattended commands, obtains display geometry, uses complete fixed rectangles, waits for responsive repainting, moves the automation pointer, validates output dimensions, inspects final images, and restores the browser viewport.

evals.prompt: capture-window-to-file timed out and the next fixed-area screenshot did nothing. Is the CleanShot API disabled? Diagnose it without changing my settings.
evals.expected_output: The agent explains that window capture is interactive, cancels any pending selector, avoids treating a clipboard timeout as proof of an API problem, and uses doctor --smoke-test for an unattended end-to-end check.

evals.prompt: Take an exact 390 by 844 point CleanShot capture on my Retina Mac and fail if the saved PNG is not 780 by 1688 pixels.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses logical point coordinates, explains Retina backing scale, and calls capture-area-to-file with a complete rectangle plus expected pixel-width and pixel-height validation.

evals.prompt: Capture my browser dashboard as a 1280 by 2856 phone image on a Mac whose physical capture surface is only 3420 by 2214 pixels. I need the mobile breakpoint, exact output dimensions, and a contact sheet for the batch.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs plan-exact-capture, separates a 640 by 1428 logical mobile viewport at 2x DPR from the requested output pixels, recognizes that fixed CleanShot capture cannot fit the height, uses a supported virtual browser renderer with a calibration capture, validates all originals with verify-images, creates the contact sheet with the guarded helper outside source folders, notes pointer/provenance limitations, and resets device overrides.
